world.title: Hormuz standoff: Trump misread his leverage and overplayed his hand
world.summary: A brief reopening of the Strait of Hormuz initially raised hopes for improved US-Iran relations, causing oil prices to fall and markets to rally. However, Iran soon closed the strait again, contradicting US assertions regarding negotiations. Experts indicate that President Trump may have miscalculated his leverage, which could negatively impact future diplomatic discussions between the two nations.

world.title: Trump's 'favourite field marshal' Asim Munir's Iran ties a red flag for US: Report
world.summary: General Asim Munir, Pakistan's Army chief and referred to by Donald Trump as his "favourite field marshal," is acting as a mediator between the US and Iran. However, his close ties to senior members of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) raise concerns among some US national security officials about the implications of his involvement in these diplomatic efforts.

world.title: 'Shooting down F-35 not small event': Iran declares victory in war, Ghalibaf says negotiations 'far from final agreement'
world.summary: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf declared victory in the war against the US and Israel, emphasizing Iran's military strength and control over the Strait of Hormuz. He criticized the US blockade and highlighted significant differences in ongoing negotiations, stating that they are "far from final agreement." Ghalibaf's remarks came during a nationally televised address amid rising tensions.

business.title: Akshay Tritiya 2026: 31% Indians feel cheated buying silver; 93% back mandatory hallmarking, reveal survey
business.summary: A survey indicates that 31% of Indian consumers feel cheated when purchasing silver, highlighting concerns about product quality. Additionally, 93% of respondents support the implementation of mandatory hallmarking to guarantee the purity and authenticity of silver items. This reflects a growing demand for improved consumer protection and quality assurance in the silver market across the country.

business.title: India’s first private gold mining project all set for launch in Andhra Pradesh
business.summary: India's first private gold mining project is set to launch in Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh. This initiative reflects the country's historical connection to gold, dating back to Emperor Ashoka, and aligns with the government's Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative. The project aims to tap into the region's mineral-rich resources, contributing to economic growth and self-reliance in gold production.

business.title: Inside the Race to Control the World’s Lithium Supply
business.summary: Global lithium demand is surging due to the rise of renewable energy and electric vehicles, prompting rapid production expansion. This increase has intensified geopolitical competition and accelerated efforts to establish sustainable and diversified supply chains. Countries and companies are racing to secure lithium resources, recognizing its critical role in the transition to cleaner energy and advanced technologies.

technology.title: Microsoft is giving Windows 11 File Explorer a speed boost, dark mode fix, and reducing explorer.exe crashes
technology.summary: Microsoft is set to release a significant update for Windows 11's File Explorer, aimed at enhancing speed and stability. The update will address issues such as 'white flashes' and reduce crashes related to explorer.exe. The company plans to prioritize core components over Copilot integration, signaling a shift in focus for Windows 11's ongoing development.

technology.title: Blackmagic Adds Apple Watch Features to iPhone Camera App
technology.summary: Blackmagic Design has released version 3.3 of its Blackmagic Camera app for iOS, featuring new integration with Apple Watch. This update allows users to remotely control their iPhone cameras, enhancing the photography experience. The addition aims to provide seamless functionality between devices, making it easier for users to capture images and videos directly from their wrist.

technology.title: Samsung's latest unlocked Galaxy S26+ US discount saves you a pretty penny
technology.summary: Samsung is providing a $175 discount on the unlocked Galaxy S26+ for customers in the United States. This promotion is available through the company's online store, making it an attractive offer for potential buyers. The discount aims to enhance the appeal of the Galaxy S26+, encouraging more customers to consider purchasing the device during this promotional period.

sports.title: IPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar back for India? Former cricketer makes bold claim
sports.summary: Former Indian cricketer Mohammad Kaif commended Bhuvneshwar Kumar for his impressive performance against Delhi Capitals, highlighting his match-winning capabilities. Kaif suggested that Kumar's form could lead to his inclusion in the Indian squad for the IPL 2026. Kumar's experience and skill as a veteran pacer may provide valuable contributions to the team in upcoming tournaments.

sports.title: Chelsea player ratings vs Man Utd: Cole Palmer & Alejandro Garnacho painfully ineffective as Liam Rosenior's blunt Blues suffer a devastating blow to their Champions League hopes
sports.summary: Chelsea faced Manchester United in a Premier League match, suffering their fourth consecutive defeat, which jeopardizes their Champions League aspirations. Player ratings highlighted Cole Palmer and Alejandro Garnacho as notably ineffective. Goalkeeper Robert Sanchez received a 6/10 for his performance, while Malo Gusto was rated 7/10. The team's overall performance was criticized, reflecting their ongoing struggles this season.

sports.title: IPL 2026: ‘Enjoy everything’- Sangakkara’s message to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after golden duck
sports.summary: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, a young talent in IPL 2026, faced criticism after a golden duck. Head coach Kumar Sangakkara addressed the situation, encouraging Sooryavanshi to enjoy the experience and maintain a positive outlook. Sangakkara emphasized the importance of patience and perspective in the game, urging the young player to focus on learning and growth amid challenges.

health.title: Weight-loss drug craze for Ozempic, Mounjaro surges in India amid safety concerns
health.summary: In India, the demand for weight-loss injectables like Ozempic and Mounjaro is increasing as consumers seek gradual weight reduction. However, healthcare professionals are expressing concerns about safety and the potential for misuse. They advocate for stricter regulations to ensure proper marketing and usage of these drugs, highlighting the need to address safety issues associated with their rising popularity.

health.title: Experts warn of ‘lean’ fatty liver disease in Indian children
health.summary: Experts in Pune have raised concerns about the rising prevalence of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), now referred to as metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD), among Indian children. On World Liver Day, hepatologists highlighted this alarming trend, emphasizing the need for increased awareness and preventive measures to address this growing public health issue in the country.

health.title: Healing the brain sans surgery The rise of TMS
health.summary: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) is a non-invasive technique for brain stimulation, utilizing a time-varying magnetic field generated by a stimulator coil placed on the scalp. This magnetic field induces an electric current in the brain tissue, leading to membrane depolarization and neural activation. TMS can produce both short-term and long-term effects on brain function and health.

science.title: Scientists Unearth 430,000-Year-Old Wooden Tools in Greece, Rewriting Evolutionary Timeline
science.summary: Scientists have discovered 430,000-year-old wooden tools at the Marathousa 1 site in Greece, marking the earliest evidence of human craftsmanship. This finding, published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, suggests that technological innovation occurred much earlier than previously thought, potentially rewriting the evolutionary timeline regarding early human tool-making and cognitive abilities.

science.title: After a Century of Mystery, Scientists Finally Discover the Cause of Blood Falls in Antarctica
science.summary: Scientists have identified the cause of Antarctica's Blood Falls, a red outflow from Taylor Glacier, after over a century of speculation. A study published in Antarctic Science reveals that the striking color results from iron-rich water under pressure, combined with high salt content. This discovery sheds light on the unique geological processes occurring beneath the glacier.

science.title: NASA Supercomputer Predictions: What They Actually Mean
science.summary: NASA employs supercomputers and climate models to simulate future scenarios for Earth and space, distinguishing their work from mystical predictions made by figures like Nostradamus. These simulations are based on scientific data and methodologies, providing insights into potential outcomes rather than prophecies. The focus is on understanding climate change and other phenomena through rigorous scientific analysis.

entertainment.title: Horoscope Today: April 19, 2026
entertainment.summary: Horoscope for April 19, 2026, advises Pisces to reassess their approach when facing obstacles. Instead of persisting in a challenging direction, consider alternative strategies such as scaling, circumventing, or finding a different path. The guidance emphasizes that the most difficult route is not always the best option, encouraging a more flexible mindset in overcoming challenges.

entertainment.title: Dhurandhar 2 Full Movie Collection: 'dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 31: ranveer singh’s film mints close to rs 1,750 cr worldwide amid competition from ‘bhooth bangla’
entertainment.summary: Ranveer Singh's film "Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge" has grossed nearly Rs 1,750 crore worldwide after 31 days in theaters. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the movie has experienced both highs and lows during its run. Despite facing competition from "Bhooth Bangla," it continues to perform strongly at the box office, achieving significant milestones and breaking records.

entertainment.title: Pisces Horoscope Today: April 19, 2026
entertainment.summary: Pisces individuals are advised to reassess their current challenges instead of persistently confronting obstacles. The horoscope suggests exploring alternative solutions, such as scaling, circumventing, or finding new paths around difficulties. This guidance encourages a shift in perspective, highlighting that the most challenging approach may not always be the best. Embracing flexibility can lead to more effective problem-solving strategies.
